title: narcissus pallidiflorus subsp. macrolobus
intro: Narcissus pallidiflorus subsp. macrolobus, also known as the pale daffodil or wild daffodil, is a subspecies of flowering plant belonging to the genus Narcissus. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Narcissus pallidiflorus subsp. macrolobus include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and easing respiratory congestion. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
